ocr.text: The data presented in the table summarizes the findings from the initial survey conducted as part of the self-reported
questionnaire initiative. This survey aimed to evaluate participant responses across a range of defined metrics, facilitating a
comprehensive analysis of the various demographic and behavioral patterns among respondents. Notably, the columns labeled
“N” and “%” provide insight into the number of participants and the corresponding percentage of the total sample size for each
Combined method(A and/or B, Gold standard)  120  11.3  97  21.9  23  3.7
Particle size (μm)  Powder part of the bone cement/MC particle (w/w)
For instance, the survey revealed that a significant proportion, represented as 84 individuals, accounted for 7.9% of the total
responses in one of the key categories, indicating a notable level of engagement with the questionnaire. Furthermore, the data
delineates the responses across varying thresholds, such as 100/0, 100/5, and 100/10, which serve to illustrate the participants'
perspectives on the core survey questions. By analyzing these metrics, researchers can draw meaningful conclusions regarding
the attitudes and behaviors of the target population, thereby enhancing the understanding of the underlying trends within the
dataset. This foundational survey serves as a critical step in informing future research and interventions within the field.
